0.q: BplMYB46 and BplMYB13 co-overexpressing transgenic plants had enhanced abilities to scavenge ROS and had decreased levels of cell death, demonstrating that the interaction between BplMYB46 and BplMYB13 could increase plant stress resistance.

under salt and osmotic stress conditions, the H2O2 levels were highest in SE3 and SE15, followed by WT, whereas OE9 and OE10 had the lowest H2O2 levels; OE lines had significantly higher SOD and POD activity.

1.q: Under salt stress, the accumulation of O2− and H2O2 in the leaves of the BpBZR1-6 OE plants was lower than that in the leaves of the WT plants... OE plants improved their salt tolerance by reducing the accumulation of ROS.
BpBZR1-6 promotes the expression of SOD and POD genes, thereby increasing SOD and POD activities; consequently, OE lines reduced H2O2 and malondialdehyde levels.

2.q: The BES1/BZR1 family represents a class of plant specific transcription factors central to BR signaling and developmental regulation.

The expression of BpBZR1-6 was significantly upregulated more than 10-fold in the leaves compared with the control treated with BR... indicated that the expression of BpBZR1-6 can be induced by salt, ABA and BRs.

3.q: BpSZA1 conferred increased tolerance to salt stress in OE lines compared with WT and Ri lines by participating in ROS scavenging, ABA biosynthesis, proline biosynthesis, and jasmonic acid (JA) responses.

4.q: BpSZA1 also participates in abscisic acid (ABA) biosynthesis, proline biosynthesis, and the ABA/jasmonic acid pathway to enhance the salt stress of B. platyphylla.

5.q: BpHOX2 could induce the expression of pyrroline‐5‐carboxylate synthase, peroxidase, and superoxide dismutase genes to improve proline levels and the reactive oxygen species scavenging capability.

6.q: Overexpression of BpNAC012 activated the expression of secondary wall-associated downstream genes by directly binding to the secondary wall NAC-binding element sites, resulting in ectopic secondary wall deposition in the stem epidermis.

8.q: OE lines had the lowest width/length ratio of the stomatal aperture... BpTCP20 induced the expression of BpRHC1 and BpMYB61... to regulate the opening and closing of stomata.

9.q: BpTCP20 can induce the expression of SODs and PODs to increase the ROS scavenging capability, leading to reduced ROS accumulation under salt stress conditions.

10.q: BpGRP1 positively regulates the salt stress response by affecting salt stress-related gene expression, enhancing ROS scavenging capability, increasing proline levels and reducing MDA levels.

11.q: BpbHLH1 can enhance the salt tolerance of birch plants by increasing the reactive oxygen species scavenging ability and inhibiting cell death.

12.q: Consistent with that of BplncSIR1, overexpression of BpNAC2 also accelerated plant growth and conferred salt tolerance... resulting in reduced ROS accumulation.

13.q: BpNAC2 binds to different cis-acting elements... resulting in reduced ROS accumulation and decreased water loss rate by stomatal closure.

14.q: SOD and POD activities were significantly higher in the BpMYB06-OE birch plants compared to pROKII-35S plants under saline–alkali stress, while these activities were lower in the SE plants

16.q: under saline–alkali stress, the stomata of the OE lines were smaller compared to those of the pROKII-35S plants, in contrast to the stomata in the SE lines, which remained open

17.q: Overexpression of BpbZIP5 correlates positively with saline-alkali stress tolerance by facilitating reactive oxygen species (ROS) elimination and maintaining ion homeostasis in B. platyphylla.

19.q: WRKY70 and NAC9 can increase resistance to saline–alkali stress by enhancing reactive oxygen species scavenging and inhibiting cell death in birch plants.

20.q: WRKY70 and NAC9 can increase resistance to saline–alkali stress by enhancing reactive oxygen species scavenging and inhibiting cell death in birch plants.

21.q: Both OE3 and OE6 lines had lower SOD and POD activities than the WT, and the Ri lines showed significantly higher SOD and POD activities than the WT plants; BpERF11 downregulates the expression of SOD and POD genes.

23.q: we identified dihydrolipoyllysine-residue acetyltransferase component of pyruvate dehydrogenase complex (BpPDCE23), an acetyltransferase that interacts with and acetylates BpTCP20 to enhance its binding to DNA motifs.

24.q: yeast expressing BplMYB6, BplMYB8, BplMYB11, BplMYB12, and BplMYB13 could grow on QDO/X-α-Gal medium, indicating that these five MYBs might interact with BplMYB46.

26.q: These results indicated that BpbZIP5 can interact with BpbZIP16 and BpbZIP20, but cannot interact with the other three BpbZIP transcription factors, nor can it interact with itself.

30.q: Expression profiling and 5′ RACE analyses provided evidence of the in vivo targeting of the BpBCP1 transcript by Bp-miR408a.

31.q: The expression levels of BpP5CS1 and BpP5CS2 were both significantly increased in the Ri lines compared with WT plants, but showed significant lower expression levels in OE lines compared with WT plants under NaCl or mannitol stress.

32.q: The expression levels of BpP5CS1 and BpP5CS2 were both significantly increased in the Ri lines compared with WT plants, but showed significant lower expression levels in OE lines compared with WT plants under NaCl or mannitol stress.

33.q: BpLEA1, BpLEA2 and BpDHN1 were upregulated in the Ri lines with higher expression levels than in the WT; however, downregulated in the OE lines, indicating that BpERF11 downregulates the expression of these genes.

37.q: proline degradation genes, BpPRODH and BpP5CDH, were greatly downregulated in Ri lines, but greatly upregulated in the OE lines compared with WT plants.

39.q: GUS activity determination showed that BpGRF3 highly activated the expression of the reporter gene in the presence of the ABRE motif.

40.q: the expression of stress-associated genes, including BpSODs, BpPODs, BpAPXs and BpP5CS1, was significantly higher in the OE lines, whereas the expression levels of these genes were significantly lower in the SE lines.

41.q: OE lines had significantly higher SOD and POD activity than the other lines... As the SOD and POD activities in these lines were altered in response to BplMYB46 expression, we examined the expression of SOD and POD genes using qRT-PCR.
